perturbation.original_text: The Holding Company has filed with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission") a registration statement on Form S-1 (File No. 333­194486) (the "Registration Statement"), containing a prospectus relating to the Subscription and Community Offering, for the registration of the Shares under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "1933 Act"), and has filed such amendments thereof and such amended prospectuses as may have been required to the date hereof. The term "Registration Statement" shall include all financial schedules and exhibits thereto, as amended, including post-effective amendments.
perturbation.changed_text: The Holding Company has filed what it believes to be a registration statement with the Securities and Exchange Commission (the "Commission") on Form S-1 (File No. 333­194486) (the "Registration Statement"), containing a prospectus relating to the Subscription and Community Offering, for the registration of the Shares under the Securities Act of 1933 (the "1933 Act"), and has filed amendments thereof and prospectuses as it deemed necessary. The term "Registration Statement" may include financial schedules and exhibits thereto.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "what it believes to be" and "as it deemed necessary," the obligation to file a proper and complete registration statement with the SEC is weakened. The phrase "may include" makes the inclusion of financial schedules and exhibits optional, which introduces further ambiguity.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 5 of the Securities Act of 1933 prohibits the offer or sale of securities without a registration statement filed with the SEC that contains all required information. The modified text introduces ambiguity about whether a compliant registration statement has actually been filed, therefore contradicting the requirements of the Securities Act.

Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails (a) Sale or delivery after sale of unregistered securities Unless a registration statement is in effect as to a security, it shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly- (1) to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or
perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 15 U.S. Code § 77e - Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails | U.S. Code | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you Quick search by citation: Title Section Go! 15 U.S. Code § 77e - Prohibitions relating to interstate commerce and the mails U.S. Code Notes Authorities (CFR) prev | next (a) Sale or delivery after sale of unregistered securities Unless a registration statement is in effect as to a security, it shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly— (1) to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or of the mails to sell such security through the use or medium of any prospectus or otherwise; or (2) to carry or cause to be carried through the mails or in interstate commerce , by any means or instruments of transportation, any such security for the purpose of sale or for delivery after sale. (b) Necessity of prospectus meeting requirements of section 77j of this title It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly— (1) to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or of the mails to carry or transmit any prospectus relating to any security with respect to which a registration statement has been filed under this subchapter, unless such prospectus meets the requirements of section 77j of this title ; or (2) to carry or cause to be carried through the mails or in interstate commerce any such security for the purpose of sale or for delivery after sale, unless accompanied or preceded by a prospectus that meets the requirements of subsection (a) of section 77j of this title . (c) Necessity of filing registration statement It shall be unlawful for any person, directly or indirectly, to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or of the mails to offer to sell or offer to buy through the use or medium of any prospectus or other
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts the Securities Act of 1933, specifically regarding the requirement for a complete and accurate registration statement. The original text affirms that the Holding Company "has filed" a registration statement and amendments as required. The changed text weakens this by stating the company filed "what it believes to be" a registration statement and made amendments "as it deemed necessary," introducing ambiguity. Furthermore, it makes the inclusion of financial schedules and exhibits optional by using "may include." According to 15 U.S. Code § 77e, "Unless a registration statement is in effect as to a security, it shall be unlawful for any person...to make use of any means or instruments of transportation or communication in interstate commerce or of the mails to sell such security." The changes introduce uncertainty about whether a compliant registration statement, containing all required information, has actually been filed, thus potentially violating this section.

perturbation.original_text: The Blue Hills Parties have filed the Prospectus and any supplemental sales literature with the Commission, the FRB, the Division and any other applicable regulator. The Prospectus, the Information Statement and all supplemental sales literature, as of the date the Registration Statement became effective and on the Closing Date referred to in Section 3, complied and will comply in all material respects with the applicable requirements of the 1933 Act Regulations, the Massachusetts Regulations and, at or prior to the time of their first use, will have received all required authorizations of the Division and the Commission and any other applicable regulator for use in final form.
perturbation.changed_text: The Blue Hills Parties have generally made an effort to file the Prospectus and supplemental sales literature with the Commission, the FRB, the Division and other regulators. The Prospectus, the Information Statement, and supplemental sales literature should substantially comply with the requirements of the 1933 Act Regulations, the Massachusetts Regulations, and will aim to receive authorizations from the Division and the Commission for use in some form.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'have filed' with 'generally made an effort to file' introduces doubt as to whether the required filings were actually completed. Substituting 'complied and will comply in all material respects' with 'should substantially comply' weakens the obligation to adhere fully to regulatory requirements, and replacing 'all required authorizations' with 'aim to receive authorizations in some form' further reduces the stringency of obtaining necessary approvals.
perturbation.law_explanation: 17 CFR § 230.424 requires the filing of prospectuses with the SEC in accordance with specific rules and timelines. The modified text introduces ambiguity by suggesting only an effort to file rather than actual filing, which contradicts the mandatory filing requirements and the specific methods outlined in the regulation.

perturbation.scraped_snippet_2: 17 CFR § 230.424 - Filing of prospectuses, number of copies. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 230.424 - Filing of prospectuses, number of copies. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 230.424 Filing of prospectuses, number of copies. (a) Except as provided in paragraph (f) of this section, five copies of every form of prospectus sent or given to any person prior to the effective date of the registration statement which varies from the form or forms of prospectus included in the registration statement as filed pursuant to § 230.402(a) of this chapter shall be filed as a part of the registration statement not later than the date such form of prospectus is first sent or given to any person: Provided, however, That only a form of prospectus that contains substantive changes from or additions to a prospectus previously filed with the Commission as part of a registration statement need be filed pursuant to this paragraph (a). (b) Ten copies of each form of prospectus purporting to comply with section 10 of the Act , except for documents constituting a prospectus pursuant to Rule 428(a) ( § 230.428(a) ) or free writing prospectuses pursuant to Rule 164 and Rule 433 ( § 230.164 and § 230.433 ), shall be filed with the Commission in the form in which it is used after the effectiveness of the registration statement and identified as required by paragraph (e) of this section; provided, however, that only a form of prospectus that contains substantive changes from or additions to a previously filed prospectus is required to be filed; Provided, further, that this paragraph (b) shall not apply in respect of a form of prospectus contained in a registration statement and relating solely to securities offered at competitive bidding, which prospectus is intended for use prior to the opening of bids. Ten copies of the form of prospectus shall be filed or transmitted for filing as f
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts 17 CFR § 230.424, which mandates the filing of prospectuses. The original text states that the Blue Hills Parties 'have filed' the prospectus, while the changed text weakens this to 'generally made an effort to file,' introducing ambiguity about whether the filing actually occurred. Additionally, the original text states that the documents 'complied and will comply in all material respects' with regulations, whereas the changed text states they 'should substantially comply,' weakening the obligation to full compliance. The original text's 'all required authorizations' is replaced with 'aim to receive authorizations in some form,' further diminishing the stringency of obtaining approvals. Snippet 2 explicitly mentions 'filing of prospectuses, number of copies,' reinforcing the mandatory nature of the filing requirement which is undermined by the change.

perturbation.original_text: In each jurisdiction where any of the Shares shall have been qualified or registered as above provided, the Holding Company will make and file such statements and reports in each fiscal period as are or may be required by the laws of such jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: In jurisdictions where the Shares are supposedly qualified or registered, the Holding Company will consider whether to make and file statements and reports in each fiscal period as maybe somewhat required.
perturbation.explanation: The original text creates a clear obligation to make and file reports as required by law. The modified text introduces ambiguity through the words "supposedly qualified," "consider whether to make and file," and "maybe somewhat required." This makes the obligation discretionary and unclear.
perturbation.law_citation: Section 305 of the Uniform Securities Act of 2002
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 305 of the Uniform Securities Act requires the filing of ongoing reports by issuers of registered securities. By making the obligation discretionary with the phrase "will consider whether to make and file," the modified text contradicts the mandatory reporting requirements mandated by this Act.
perturbation.justification: The modified text significantly weakens the legal obligation to file reports, as the original text has a clear obligation to file the reports. The changed text uses softening language like "supposedly qualified," "consider whether to make and file," and "maybe somewhat required." This makes the filing of reports discretionary, which contradicts the mandatory reporting requirements as stated in the Law Explanation referencing Section 305 of the Uniform Securities Act. Since no scraped snippets were available to further strengthen this assessment, it is based on the clear discrepancy between the original and changed text, and the explanation that references a potential legal violation.
